question.text: When is buying a put option generally PREFERABLE to short selling futures for expressing a bearish view?

answer.text: When the trader wants to limit maximum loss to a known premium amount
When the trader wants unlimited profit potential only
When the trader has a very strong conviction and wants maximum leverage
When the trader expects the market to stay flat

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Put option advantages over short futures: 1. LIMITED LOSS: max loss = premium paid (known upfront) 2. No margin calls: even if market temporarily rises, no additional capital needed 3. No forced liquidation: can hold through adverse moves
Short futures disadvantage: unlimited loss potential, margin calls, forced liquidation risk.
**The Trap:** Short futures provide more leverage and no time decay. Puts have time decay working against them.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Risk-averse bearish traders prefer puts. Aggressive traders may prefer short futures.
**Key Takeaway:** Choose puts over short futures when risk management (limited loss) is the priority.
